title: Ohio Gubernatorial Candidate: Stop regulating abortion and abolish it!
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States profiles 2022 Ohio Republican gubernatorial candidate Joe Blystone, whose town hall remarks called for abolishing rather than regulating abortion, and contrasts him with incumbent Mike DeWine and likely opponent Jim Renacci. The article frames Blystone as a candidate abolitionists nationwide should watch and criticizes the toothless Ohio Heartbeat Bill.
audiences: Ohio voter weighing 2022 gubernatorial primary candidates
Pro-life advocate evaluating the Heartbeat Bill strategy
Abolitionist activist looking for political allies
Christian church leader discerning candidate endorsements

questions.question: Who is Joe Blystone and why does Free the States consider his abortion stance noteworthy?
questions.audience: Ohio voter weighing 2022 gubernatorial primary candidates
questions.answer: Joe Blystone is one of the 2022 Republican candidates for Ohio governor. At a Cincinnati town hall on April 27, he was asked about abortion and called for abolition rather than regulation, a position Free the States describes as 'amazing' and aligned with abolitionism.
questions.quote: Among the 2022 Republican candidates for Ohio governor is Joe Blystone. During a townhall event in Cincinnati on April 27, he was asked about his position on abortion. His response was amazing

questions.question: How does Blystone distinguish 'pro-life' from 'abolitionist' in his town hall remarks?
questions.audience: Pro-life advocate evaluating the Heartbeat Bill strategy
questions.answer: Blystone defines being pro-life as merely regulating murder, and argues Ohio must instead abolish abortion outright rather than continuing to manage how babies are killed.
questions.quote: Here's what pro-life is. Pro-life is the regulation of murder in this state. That's all it is. We need to stop regulating the murder of babies, and we need to abolish abortion in this state. Period.

questions.question: What is Blystone's specific critique of the Ohio Heartbeat Bill?
questions.audience: Pro-life advocate evaluating the Heartbeat Bill strategy
questions.answer: Blystone says the Heartbeat Bill sounds appealing but lacks any real enforcement power and continues to permit the killing of more than 23,000 Ohio babies each year.
questions.quote: The Heartbeat Bill allows 23 thousand-plus babies to be killed in this state every year, and I won't have it.

questions.question: Why does Free the States agree that Heartbeat Bills are functionally useless?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist looking for political allies
questions.answer: The author argues that Ohio's Heartbeat Bill, like all such bills, was enjoined before it could take effect and will likely never be enforced unless Ohio defies Supreme Court rulings, meaning the legislation has not actually stopped any abortions.
questions.quote: The Ohio Heartbeat Bill, like all Heartbeat Bills, was enjoined before taking effect, and is unlikely to ever be enforced unless Ohio pushes back on the supreme court's tyranny. Babies are still murdered in Ohio.

questions.question: How did Mike DeWine campaign on abortion and what did he do once in office?
questions.audience: Ohio voter weighing 2022 gubernatorial primary candidates
questions.answer: DeWine ran in 2018 as a pro-life candidate supporting the Heartbeat Bill and signed it during his first session. After signing, he refused to pursue further abolition, telling Ohioans the state should wait to see how the courts handled the bill.
questions.quote: Dewine ran in 2018 on a pro-life platform of supporting the Heartbeat Bill, which he signed during his first session as governor. After the passage of the bill, Dewine said he wasn't going to work to abolish abortion because Ohio had to wait and see what the courts did with the Heartbeat Bill.

questions.question: How does the article translate DeWine's stated post-Heartbeat Bill strategy?
questions.answer: Free the States interprets DeWine's promise to focus on bills already passed as a euphemism for inaction on abortion, accusing him of using procedural deference to avoid pursuing abolition.
questions.quote: "Focusing on the bills we've already passed" is code for not doing anything to abolish abortion.

questions.question: What did Cleveland Right to Life do regarding their previous award to DeWine?
questions.audience: Pro-life advocate evaluating the Heartbeat Bill strategy
questions.answer: Cleveland Right to Life revoked the Champion for Life Award they had given DeWine in 2015 because of his repeated alignment with the abortion industry, a notable break given that the group itself is not abolitionist.
questions.quote: Dewine has gone out of his way in numerous ways to side with the abortion industry according to Cleveland Right to Life, which resulted in the group revoking the Champion for Life Award they gave him in 2015. Cleveland Right to Life is not an abolitionist organization, and even they are fed up with Dewine.

questions.question: How does the article characterize Jim Renacci's likely candidacy?
questions.audience: Ohio voter weighing 2022 gubernatorial primary candidates
questions.answer: Free the States dismisses former Congressman Jim Renacci as uninterested in the abortion issue, noting that his website does not mention abortion at all, which the author treats as evidence he will do nothing to save Ohio babies.
questions.quote: Renacci doesn't mention abortion anywhere on his website, indicating a complete lack of interest in saving the children being murdered in Ohio.

questions.question: Who is Joe Blystone's running mate and what did she say about him?
questions.answer: Blystone announced he would campaign with Lt. Governor candidate Joanna Swallen, a business owner and activist from Canton, who described him in their announcement video as not merely pro-life but an abolitionist.
questions.quote: Joe would tell you that he is not only pro-life, he would be an abolitionist.

questions.question: How are abolitionist organizers engaging with Blystone on the ground?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist looking for political allies
questions.answer: Free the States' Rachel Burkey and Ohio activist Sarah Cleveland have personally met with Blystone and report that he is rapidly grasping abolitionist principles, prompting them to recommend him as a candidate worth national attention.
questions.quote: Abolitionists on the ground in Ohio, including Free the States' Rachel Burkey and Ohio activist Sarah Cleveland, have met with Blystone in recent weeks and are impressed with how quickly he is grasping abolitionism. He is definitely a candidate who abolitionists around the country should be keeping an eye on.

questions.question: What concrete actions does the article ask readers to take after reading?
questions.audience: Christian church leader discerning candidate endorsements
questions.answer: The article closes with three calls to action: signing up for Free the States email updates to bypass big tech censorship, becoming an activist who converts pro-lifers to abolitionism using the Are You An Abolitionist pamphlet, and donating to fund the movement.
questions.quote: Be an activist! Convert people in your community to abolitionism. The best brief resource in the Abolitionist Movement for turning pro-lifers into abolitionists is the Are You An Abolitionist pamphlet.

related.title: Pastor and Gubernatorial Candidate Dan Fisher Responds to Oklahoma Baptist Leaders Opposing SB13
related.reason: Another profile of an abolitionist gubernatorial candidate (Oklahoma's Dan Fisher) confronting pro-life opposition, paralleling Blystone's Ohio campaign.

related.title: Tennessee Fetal Heartbeat Bill—A Step in the Wrong Direction
related.reason: Provides the broader abolitionist case against Heartbeat Bills that Blystone echoes when criticizing Ohio's enjoined version.

related.title: Abolitionist Perspective on the Texas Heartbeat Bill: Three Quick Observations
related.reason: Reinforces the article's claim that Heartbeat Bills are toothless regulation rather than abolition by analyzing the Texas equivalent.

related.title: OK Legislature Sends 3 Useless Pro-Life Bills to Governor Stitt
related.reason: Mirrors the article's framing of incremental pro-life legislation as procedural cover that lets abortion continue.

related.title: Idaho Pro-Life State Senator Says Ending Abortion “Destroys the Pro-Life Movement”
related.reason: Illustrates the same pro-life-versus-abolitionist tension that Blystone calls out at his Cincinnati town hall.

related.title: Immediate, Not Gradual Abolition of Abortion
related.reason: Lays out the philosophical case for immediatism that Blystone embodies when he refuses to settle for regulating murder.

related.title: June V. Russo Must Be the Death Knell of Pro-Life Judicial Supremacy and Incrementalism
related.reason: Expands on the Supreme Court 'tyranny' the article references when explaining why Ohio's Heartbeat Bill remains enjoined.
